description: Standard: feature on prison education programs

input.central_argument: Prison education programs reduce recidivism by 43% but are being defunded at the state level, creating a system that punishes people twice
key_findings: Inmates who complete degree programs while incarcerated are 43% less likely to return to prison within five years
State funding for prison education has been cut by 60% since 2010 in the region studied
One program at a medium-security facility has a 92% employment rate among graduates
The corrections officers' union opposes the programs, citing security concerns about classroom gatherings
The cost per inmate of the education program is $5,200 per year vs. $38,000 per year to incarcerate
input.sources: Program director, two current inmates enrolled in the program, a corrections officer who supports it reluctantly, a state legislator who voted to cut funding, a criminologist who studies recidivism

input.central_argument: A local bakery that has operated for 80 years is closing because the building was sold to a developer
key_findings: The bakery has been in the same family for three generations
The building was sold for $2.8 million to a developer planning luxury condos
Regular customers organized a petition with 4,000 signatures
input.sources: The bakery owner, two longtime customers, the developer

description: European: investigation into EU fishing quotas

input.central_argument: EU fishing quotas meant to protect fish stocks are being systematically undermined by industrial fleets using a legal loophole that allows quota trading between member states
key_findings: Dutch-flagged supertrawlers now hold fishing quotas originally allocated to small-scale fishers in France and Portugal
Quota trading is technically legal under EU fisheries regulation but was never intended to enable industrial consolidation
Coastal communities in Brittany have lost 70% of their allocated quota in the past decade
The European Commission has twice proposed reform but was blocked by the Netherlands and Denmark
input.sources: French inshore fisherman, Dutch fleet operator, EU fisheries policy official, marine biologist, a commissioner's spokesperson
